svg.text: Sonny — Wiring: Scenario A (build now) + Scenario B (stretch)
PCA9685 servos + 1 center HC-SR04 (solid) — left/right HC-SR04 added later (dashed). Level shifter shown for ECHO 5V→3.3V.

g.text: ━━━ SOLID GREEN: build for Scenario A (1 ultrasonic)
┄┄┄ DASHED ORANGE: add for Scenario B (3 ultrasonics)
Black = power/UART/I2C, Blue = signal nets, Red = +5V, Black = GND
Motor PWM + IR + ultrasonic + buzzer · UART2 to Pi
• TRIG pins: NO shifter needed (3.3V drives
• ECHO pins MUST go through shifter
Adding L+R later requires NO firmware change.
(MOSFET type — VCCA / VCCB with GND on both sides)
⚠ TRIG lines bypass this shifter — wired direct from ESP3.3V to HC-SR04 TRIG (no translation needed).
Build order (Scenario A first — DO NOT skip steps)
1. Trim LM2596 trim-pot to 5.0V with multimeter BEFORE connecting PCA9685 servo rail. Servos die at 6V+.
2. Establish common GND first: Pi GND ↔ ESP GND ↔ LM2596 GND ↔ shifter GND ↔ all HC-SR04 GND. One star ground saves hours of debugging.
3. Wire shifter VCCA (3.3V from ESP) and VCCB (5V from ESP/buck). Verify with multimeter that VCCA reads 3.3V and VCCB reads 5.0V before connecting any signal.
4. Wire CENTER HC-SR04: VCC→5V, GND→GND, TRIG→ESP GPIO 18 (direct), ECHO→shifter B1 (HV side), then shifter A1 (LV side)→ESP GPIO 1.
5. Power up. Watch ESP serial: should see DIST_C:XX.X at 10Hz. Wave hand 10–100cm — value should change smoothly.
6. If center sensor reads stable, STOP and ship Scenario A. Don't add L+R unless you have ≥30 minutes of bench time.
Upgrade to Scenario B (only if A is rock-solid)
7. Wire LEFT HC-SR04: VCC→5V, GND→GND, TRIG→GPIO 19, ECHO→shifter B2→A2→GPIO 20.
8. Wire RIGHT HC-SR04: VCC→5V, GND→GND, TRIG→GPIO 40, ECHO→shifter B3→A3→GPIO 39.
9. ESP32 firmware already reads all 3 sensors and broadcasts DIST_L / DIST_C / DIST_R — no re-flash needed.
10. Drop the --no-ultrasonic flag from launch when running Scenario B.
Why this level shifter is risky for HC-SR04 (mentioned on Apr 22)
• The MOSFET shifter pulls HIGH via a passive 10kΩ pull-up. RC time constant ~100ns. HC-SR04 echo edges are ~50ns rise/fall.
• Marginal — usually works, sometimes intermittent. If you see DIST_C: -1.0 (timeout) randomly, the shifter is the suspect.
• Backup plan: build a resistor divider on each ECHO instead. 1kΩ ECHO→GPIO, 2kΩ GPIO→GND. 5V × 2/(1+2) = 3.33V. Sharp edges, no pull-up RC.
• 4× SG90 stall: 4 × 0.6A = 2.4A peak. Don't command all 4 servos to extremes simultaneously. Stagger arm + head animations.
• HC-SR04 ×3 active: ~45mA total at 5V. Negligible. They draw current pulses on TRIG, idle is <5mA each.
